body_ache: poora badan dard kar raha hai

disease_descriptions.Malaria: A mosquito-borne disease caused by Plasmodium parasites. Common in tropical regions of India. Treatable with antimalarial drugs.
disease_descriptions.Dengue Fever: A viral infection spread by Aedes mosquitoes. Can range from mild to severe. Requires medical monitoring and supportive care.
disease_descriptions.Typhoid: A bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi, spread through contaminated food and water. Requires antibiotic treatment.
disease_descriptions.Tuberculosis: A bacterial lung disease caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Highly treatable with a 6-month antibiotic course under DOTS programme.
disease_descriptions.COVID-19: A viral respiratory illness caused by SARS-CoV-2. Symptoms range from mild to severe. Consult a doctor if breathlessness develops.
disease_descriptions.Chickenpox: A highly contagious viral infection causing itchy blisters. Usually mild in children; can be severe in adults.
disease_descriptions.Pneumonia: Infection causing inflammation in the lungs. Can be bacterial or viral. Requires medical treatment.
disease_descriptions.Hepatitis A: A liver infection caused by the Hepatitis A virus, spread through contaminated food and water. Usually resolves on its own.
disease_descriptions.Hepatitis B: A serious liver infection caused by the Hepatitis B virus. Can become chronic and lead to liver damage.
disease_descriptions.Cholera: A severe bacterial infection causing profuse watery diarrhoea and dehydration. Requires immediate rehydration therapy.
disease_descriptions.Influenza: A seasonal viral respiratory illness. Rest, fluids, and antiviral drugs help recovery.
disease_descriptions.Chikungunya: A mosquito-borne viral disease causing severe joint pain. Supportive treatment relieves symptoms.
disease_descriptions.Diabetes: A metabolic condition where the body cannot regulate blood sugar. Requires lifestyle changes and medication.
disease_descriptions.Hypertension: Persistently high blood pressure. Often called the silent killer. Controlled with medication and lifestyle changes.
disease_descriptions.Hypothyroidism: Underactive thyroid gland producing insufficient hormones. Managed with daily thyroid hormone replacement.
disease_descriptions.Hyperthyroidism: Overactive thyroid gland. Treated with medications, radioactive iodine, or surgery.
disease_descriptions.Gastroenteritis: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, usually from infection. Rehydration and rest are key.
disease_descriptions.Jaundice: Yellowing of skin and eyes due to excess bilirubin. Often a symptom of liver or bile duct problems.
disease_descriptions.Peptic Ulcer: Sores in the lining of the stomach or small intestine. Treated with antacids and antibiotics.
disease_descriptions.Irritable Bowel Syndrome: A chronic digestive disorder causing abdominal pain and altered bowel habits. Managed with diet and stress reduction.
disease_descriptions.Asthma: A chronic respiratory condition causing airway inflammation and breathing difficulty. Inhalers provide relief.
disease_descriptions.COPD: A chronic inflammatory lung disease causing obstructed airflow. Smoking is the primary cause. Requires ongoing management.
disease_descriptions.Bronchitis: Inflammation of the bronchial tubes. Acute bronchitis usually resolves; chronic bronchitis requires ongoing care.
disease_descriptions.Migraine: A neurological condition causing intense recurring headaches. Medication and lifestyle changes help.
disease_descriptions.Vertigo: A sensation of dizziness and spinning. Often caused by inner ear problems. Usually treatable.
disease_descriptions.Epilepsy: A neurological disorder causing recurrent seizures. Managed with anti-epileptic medications.
disease_descriptions.Common Cold: A viral upper respiratory infection. Rest, fluids, and OTC remedies provide relief.
disease_descriptions.Urinary Tract Infection: A bacterial infection of the urinary system. Treated with antibiotics.
disease_descriptions.Anemia: Low haemoglobin levels in the blood. Treated with iron supplements or addressing the underlying cause.
disease_descriptions.Allergy: An immune response to a harmless substance. Managed with antihistamines and avoiding triggers.
disease_descriptions.Dengue Hemorrhagic Fever: A severe form of dengue causing bleeding and organ damage. Requires immediate hospitalisation.
disease_descriptions.Leptospirosis: A bacterial infection spread through animal urine. Treatable with antibiotics.
disease_descriptions.Kala-Azar: A parasitic disease transmitted by sandfly bites, common in Bihar, Jharkhand, and UP. Requires specialised treatment.
disease_descriptions.Japanese Encephalitis: A viral brain infection spread by mosquitoes. Preventable by vaccination.
disease_descriptions.Myocardial Infarction: A heart attack. Blockage of blood supply to the heart. CALL EMERGENCY SERVICES IMMEDIATELY.
disease_descriptions.Stroke: A brain attack caused by blocked or ruptured blood vessels. CALL EMERGENCY SERVICES IMMEDIATELY.
disease_descriptions.Kidney Disease: Reduced kidney function. Requires ongoing management to slow progression.
disease_descriptions.Liver Cirrhosis: Scarring of the liver from long-term damage. Requires medical management.
disease_descriptions.Psoriasis: A chronic skin condition causing red, scaly patches. Managed with topical and systemic treatments.
disease_descriptions.Eczema: A condition causing inflamed, itchy skin. Managed with moisturisers and topical steroids.
disease_descriptions.Appendicitis: Inflammation of the appendix requiring immediate surgical removal. SEEK EMERGENCY CARE.
disease_descriptions.Meningitis: Inflammation of the brain and spinal cord membranes. SEEK EMERGENCY CARE IMMEDIATELY.
